response.justification: Candidate has genuinely relevant geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map web GIS) and has worked on low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby. However, the core technical requirement — modern C++ or Rust — is a significant gap: C/C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments over 10 years ago plus a bachelor thesis, and there is no Rust experience. Recent work is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. No direct navigation, routing, or map-tiling experience. Worth a screening call only if the team is open to a strong generalist who would need to ramp up substantially on C++.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Distributed, low-latency systems background (millions of req/s at Sociomantic)
Polyglot systems-leaning engineer (DLang, C/C++, Java) comfortable across stacks
concerns: C++ experience is shallow and over a decade old; no Rust experience
No navigation, routing, or map-tiling pipeline experience
Recent roles are predominantly web/full-stack TypeScript, not systems programming
